content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

April 18, 1997

Mr. James R. Sutton
Law Offices of Nielsen, Merksamer
Parrinello, Mueller & Naylor
591 Redwood Highway, #4000
Mill Valley, California 94941

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-97-130

Dear Mr. Sutton:

This letter is a response to your request for informal advice regarding the provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

You ask if the "blackout" period in Section 85305(a) applies to contributions from a
candidate's personal funds to his or her campaign. It does not. We have advised that the off-
year ban in Section 85305 does not apply to a candidate's contributions of his or her personal
funds to his or her controlled committee. (Rosenthal Advice Letter, No. I-97-063a, copy
enclosed.)

If you have any questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Luisa Menchaca
Senior Staff Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:LM:ak

¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations. Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

sections.question: You ask if the "blackout" period in Section 85305(a) applies to contributions from a candidate's personal funds to his or her campaign.
sections.conclusion: It does not. We have advised that the off-year ban in Section 85305 does not apply to a candidate's contributions of his or her personal funds to his or her controlled committee.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the contribution blackout period in Section 85305(a) applies to a candidate's personal fund contributions to their own campaign committee.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The blackout period in Section 85305(a) does not apply to contributions from a candidate's personal funds to their controlled committee.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Clear informal advice letter with identifiable question and conclusion sections. References prior related advice letter (Rosenthal, No. I-97-063a). Letter includes standard disclaimer that informal assistance does not provide immunity under Section 83114.

embedding.summary: FPPC informal advice letter responding to a question about whether contribution blackout periods under Section 85305(a) apply to a candidate's personal fund contributions to their own campaign committee. The FPPC advises that such personal contributions are exempt from the off-year contribution ban.
